benchmark.title: Regional Benchmark
benchmark.description: World Bank, OECD, and trade-context benchmarks for reading Uzbekistan-USA opportunities against peers.

visits.description: Bilateral visit history, active windows, delegations, anchors, and source-backed diplomatic momentum.

agreements.title: Bilateral Agreements
agreements.description: Registry and analysis of Uzbekistan-USA agreements, legal anchors, categories, source status, and timeline.

events.title: Events & Calendar
events.description: Upcoming and historical events for Uzbekistan-USA coordination, delegations, business forums, and policy moments.

commitments.title: Actions & Commitments
commitments.description: Operational register of commitments linked to visits, agreements, owners, status, and follow-up risk.

compliance.title: U.S. Compliance
compliance.description: Source-backed compliance reference for sanctions, export controls, tariffs, FATF context, and U.S. market-entry checks.

grants.title: Grants & Assistance
grants.description: Internal grant register and U.S. foreign assistance records with source separation, methodology, and reconciliation notes.

news.title: Strategic Signals
news.description: Filtered news and policy signals for Uzbekistan-USA diplomacy, trade, investment, minerals, security, and economy.

contacts.title: Contacts Directory
contacts.description: Directory of agencies, coordination bodies, embassies, councils, and staff contacts for bilateral work.

counterparts.description: Briefing registry for U.S. officials, congressional stakeholders, institutions, topics, engagement history, and sources.

counterpart.title: {name} - Counterpart Briefing
counterpart.description: Source-aware counterpart briefing with engagement history, role context, topics, committees, and preparation notes.

prepare.title: Visit Preparation
prepare.description: Operational visit-preparation workspace for pipeline status, documents, logistics, outcomes, and post-visit reconciliation.

staff.title: Staff KPI
staff.description: Internal staff KPI tracker, milestones, operational cadence, completion tracking, and source-marked demo status.

adminLogin.title: Admin Login
adminLogin.description: Secure administrator sign-in for protected operations and internal platform management.

sectorsPage.title: Sector opportunities
sectorsPage.subtitle: Briefing-quality reads on the {count} cooperation lanes with the strongest U.S.-Uzbekistan leverage

card.title: Where bilateral cooperation has the most leverage
card.sub: Each card carries a signal, a why-it-matters, and a next-question to push the conversation forward in a meeting

benchmarkPage.title: Regional benchmark
benchmarkPage.subtitle: Uzbekistan vs. Central Asia and South Caucasus on U.S.-relevant metrics

demo.tooltip: Demonstration data. To be updated from {source}.
demo.banner: This section contains demonstration data. Real data will be supplied by the responsible agencies.

overview.title: Overview
overview.subtitle: Key indicators, active agenda and signals requiring attention

hero.subtitle: Bilateral economic and diplomatic coordination — source-backed metrics, momentum signals, and recommended next actions.
hero.mandate: Source confidence remains visible; demo and pending values are separated from quote-safe figures.

flow.what: The relationship is expanding in absolute trade volume, while the balance remains structurally tilted toward U.S. exports to Uzbekistan.
flow.why: Senior users should treat export diversification and market-access work as the main trade agenda, not only the headline turnover number.
flow.how: Use this as the first chart in briefings, then open Trade & Economic Flows for methodology and product-level detail.

sectors.what: Investment value clusters in a small set of sectors; some rows are verified and others are coordination or demo records.
sectors.why: The priority is to separate quote-safe opportunities from records that still need owners, source IDs, or U.S. counterpart mapping.
sectors.how: Use the sector grid to choose the next due-diligence lane, then go to Investment & Privatization for action fields.

monthly.what: Monthly goods data gives the near-term pulse, but it should not override the annual quote-ready series.
monthly.why: It helps staff see inflection points and timing before official annual narratives are updated.
monthly.how: Use as an early-warning chart, not as the sole external headline.

risk.what: The risk view joins commitments, agreements, milestones, and visit readiness into one operating queue.
risk.why: Executives need to see what blocks outcomes, not just what has already been announced.
risk.how: Open each item to work the source registry, commitment owner, or visit-prep page.

grants.what: Grant and assistance records show soft-power and development lanes, but accounting systems differ.
grants.why: Do not combine UZ-side internal grants, USAID records, and ForeignAssistance.gov obligations without reconciliation.
grants.how: Use the donut to orient the portfolio, then keep methodology notes visible on the grants page.

overview.detailedAnalysisSub: Trade, sectors, partners, risks and the 90-day horizon

relationshipPillars.title: Relationship pillars
relationshipPillars.sub: Diplomacy, trade, investment, security, education, mobility, and regional strategy - each with a next move

diplomacy.title: Diplomacy
diplomacy.narrative: The relationship has enough high-level cadence to support an operating rhythm, but agreement-level metadata still needs owner validation.
diplomacy.action: Convert the treaty base into a searchable agreement-level register.

trade.title: Trade
trade.narrative: Trade analysis is strongest when UZ Stat and U.S. Census are shown side by side with explicit methodology boundaries.
trade.action: Automate monthly Census refresh and keep UZ-side figures source-owner approved.

investment.title: Investment
investment.narrative: The investment portfolio is the strongest executive story, but demo rows must remain separated before external use.
investment.action: Prioritize DFC/EXIM-ready projects and require sponsor, owner, value, status, and source fields.

security-minerals.title: Security and minerals
security-minerals.narrative: Critical minerals connect economic, security, and regional strategy; this should be treated as a standing watchlist.
security-minerals.action: Create a minerals tracker with counterpart, concession, financing, export-control, and infrastructure dependencies.

education-aid.title: Education and aid
education-aid.narrative: Development assistance and education are useful soft-power indicators, but their accounting systems must stay separate.
education-aid.action: Add Open Doors education data and normalize ForeignAssistance.gov sector summaries.

people-mobility.title: People and mobility
people-mobility.narrative: Visa-free travel and tourism growth can become a distinctive relationship metric if paired with visa/admissions data.
people-mobility.action: Add State visa statistics, DHS admissions, and Open Doors education flows.

regional-strategy.title: Regional strategy
regional-strategy.narrative: The benchmark page is valuable, but it should be connected to concrete policy implications and regional competition.
regional-strategy.action: Refresh macro benchmark data through World Bank WDI and add policy implication notes per metric.

sourceQuality.liveSub: Public APIs are wired as optional server connectors; private operations need credentials.
sourceQuality.thresholdNote: Source-age scale: <=45 days fresh, 45-120 days watch, >120 days stale. The topbar shows the last platform review (graded by the oldest source), not a claim that every source is fresh.

reasons.internal: Attached/internal source, {age}; confirm owner sign-off before publication.
reasons.stale: Public source is {age}; refresh before executive use.
reasons.partner: Public partner/derived source, {age}; keep source note visible.

connectorUse.census-hs-trade: Merchandise trade, HS structure, state/port exports where query limits allow.
connectorUse.world-bank-wdi: Regional benchmark, GDP, population, FDI, macro context.
connectorUse.bea-services: Services trade, international transactions, investment position, and USTR services-figure validation.
connectorUse.imf-data: FX, balance-of-payments, reserves, and macro-financial context after SDMX dataset mapping.
connectorUse.foreign-assistance: U.S. foreign assistance obligations/disbursements by year, agency, and sector.

monitor.title: Operational data monitor
monitor.databaseConfigured: Operational database is configured for this environment.
monitor.databaseStatic: Static mode is active; write operations are not available.

trade.title: Trade
trade.subtitle: External trade with the USA, 2017 – Jan 2026
trade.source: Source: State Statistics Committee of Uzbekistan

sections.annualSub: All figures USD millions · State Statistics Committee of Uzbekistan
sections.methodologyTitle: Dual methodology · UZ Stat ↔ U.S. Census
sections.methodologySub: Same flow, different rules — mirror discrepancy is real and useful
sections.methodologySummary: Side-by-side comparison of the UZ Stat and U.S. Census trade methodologies. Open it to view both series together.
sections.monthlySub: Bars: U.S. exports / imports · Line: net balance — last 26 months
sections.topExportTitle: Top UZ export categories to U.S.
sections.topExportSub: USD millions · 2025 · State Statistics Committee structure
sections.topImportTitle: Top UZ import categories from U.S.
sections.topImportSub: USD millions · 2025 · State Statistics Committee structure

annual.what: The annual table is the quote-safe backbone for turnover, exports, imports, and balance.
annual.why: It gives officials a consistent headline series and prevents accidental mixing with U.S. reporter-side data.
annual.how: Use this for external annual statements, then cite methodology notes when comparing against Census or Comtrade.
annual.source: USD millions, State Statistics Committee of Uzbekistan.

methodology.what: UZ Stat, U.S. Census, and Comtrade can describe the same flow differently.
methodology.why: Mirror gaps are not necessarily errors; they can indicate valuation, timing, reporter, or classification differences.
methodology.how: Use this chart when an analyst or policymaker asks why two public sources do not match.

flow.what: The line view reveals direction and volatility across the full 2017-2025 relationship.
flow.why: It quickly shows whether exports, imports, and turnover are moving together or diverging.
flow.how: Use for executive briefings before drilling into monthly and product tables.

monthly.what: Monthly U.S. Census data shows near-term trade pulse and seasonal movement.
monthly.why: It is useful for monitoring recent changes, but it should not replace the quote-ready annual UZ Stat view.
monthly.how: Use as a monitoring chart and keep annual methodology visible for external claims.
monthly.source: USD values, U.S. Census reporter-side goods series.

structure.what: The structure charts identify which categories carry the bilateral goods relationship.
structure.why: They connect policy conversations to concrete product lanes and market-entry questions.
structure.how: Use to choose where HS/Comtrade or ITC analysis should go deeper.

categories.what: The ranked tables make the biggest export and import categories easy to quote and compare.
categories.why: They prevent the trade page from becoming only a time-series dashboard.
categories.how: Use the rank tables for meeting notes, then open advanced analysis for HS-level precision.

ranking.exporters: Top UZ exporters to the USA

advanced.title: Advanced Trade Analysis
advanced.subtitle: HS-6, mirror discrepancies, HS-2 treemap, ITC products, services, and export-potential exhibits
advanced.preservesText: granular product analysis, reporter-side discrepancies, Trade Map prioritization views, and services data — detailed exhibits for in-depth analysis.

cards.hs6Sub: Top-25 product codes for Uzbekistan-U.S. bilateral trade - granular detail beyond StatCom HS-2
cards.mirrorSub: 2024 · same flow seen from both sides at HS-6 level - top-20 by absolute gap
cards.sparkSub: Top-10 HS-6 codes 2021-2025 with CAGR - surfaces breakout positions
cards.treemapTitle: Trade structure by HS-2 chapters · treemap
cards.treemapSub: Harmonized System chapters - coarse trade decomposition by aggregated categories
cards.itcTitle: ITC Trade Map · 2024 deep view with momentum
cards.itcSub: HS-6 with pre-computed Share % and 5-year compound growth - sortable by value, share, or growth
cards.servicesSub: Uzbekistan-reported services exports to the U.S. - what Comtrade cannot show
cards.exhibitsTitle: ITC analytical exhibits · export potential and diversification
cards.exhibitsSub: Trade Map's EPI and Diversification Indicator outputs - strategic prioritization views

commitments.title: Commitments registry
commitments.subtitle: Tracking every commitment from summits, meetings and MoUs

investments.title: Investment portfolio
investments.subtitle: Projects with U.S. partners across sectors and regions of Uzbekistan

credibilityCards.verifiedText: Safest externally quotable investment figure. Internal and illustrative rows are excluded.
credibilityCards.pendingText: Useful for coordination, but requires owner sign-off before executive publication.
credibilityCards.demoText: Do not quote as a real pipeline. These rows remain marked, searchable, and filterable.
credibilityCards.portfolioSub: {count} total rows preserved · verified, pending, and demo values are separated below

credibility.label: Credibility rule:
credibility.text: headline investment totals separate verified public records, source-backed records pending owner review, and illustrative demo pipeline rows. Demo values remain useful for interface testing, but they are not official pipeline totals.

filters.minValueAria: Minimum investment value in millions of dollars
filters.searchAria: Search investments by title or partner

privatization.emptyDescription: The platform is ready for a privatization pipeline, but no verified asset-level records are loaded yet. Required fields include asset name, sector, ownership status, transaction type, stage, timeline, government counterpart, value range, U.S. investor relevance, source IDs, risk level, and next step.
privatization.schemaFields: asset name · sector · owner/counterpart · transaction type · stage · value range · U.S. investor relevance · source IDs · risk · next action
privatization.safeState: Safe empty state: no company or asset names are invented. Add records only after owner approval and source IDs are available.

drawer.meaningDemo: This row should be used for workflow demonstration only until the responsible agency supplies a source-backed project record.
drawer.meaningVerified: This row can support executive briefing, while still retaining its source badge and as-of context.
drawer.meaningInternal: This row is useful for internal pipeline tracking, but should not be quoted publicly until owner review and source confirmation are complete.

status.operating: Capture aftercare needs, expansion signals, and measurable outcomes.
status.construction: Confirm implementation milestone, permits, financing, and delivery risks.
status.agreed: Turn agreement into an implementation owner, timetable, and source-backed milestone.
status.negotiation: Clarify decision maker, commercial terms, bottlenecks, and next meeting.
status.mou: Convert MoU into due-diligence pack, counterpart owner, and decision deadline.
status.paused: Document blocker, reactivation condition, and whether the row should remain visible.

blockers.ownerReview: Owner review required before external publication

usRelevance.confirm: U.S. company/investor fit requires owner confirmation.

publication.verified: Can support executive briefing when shown with source badge and as-of context.
publication.demo: Illustrative workflow row only; do not cite as a real project or official pipeline value.
publication.internal: Useful for internal coordination; owner review and source confirmation required before publication.

empty.title: No projects
empty.description: No records match the active filters in this status.

view.searchAria: Search grants by title or donor
view.emptyDescription: Clear the search field or choose another sector to return to the full grants register.

foreignAssistance.preliminaryNote: FY2025 bar is preliminary (partial reporting per ForeignAssistance.gov).
foreignAssistance.methodology: Methodology: total obligations basis (not net disbursements). Source: ForeignAssistance.gov country-page figures, cross-checked with the USAFacts aggregation. Yearly totals can shift by 5-10% as agencies revise prior-year reporting.

strategic.title: United States — Uzbekistan footprint
strategic.sub: State GDP, population, students, missions, and planned bilateral visits — coordinated through the Situational Center

panel.title: Uzbekistan in the United States
panel.noteText: The map combines hard economic weight (state GDP, population), demand signal (students from UZ), and diplomatic presence (open and planned missions). Use it to brief which states deserve the next visit, mission opening, or trade-promotion event.

loadGate.title: Interactive map ready on demand
loadGate.body: The state-level footprint map uses a heavier browser runtime. Load it when geographic detail matters; the summary panel above is always available.

admin.title: Administration
admin.hideDemoDesc: Hides every component that contains is_demo=true values.
admin.presentationDesc: Removes DEMO badges and dotted underlines but keeps the data.
admin.themeDesc: Choose the reading surface for this browser.
admin.registryDesc: Master list of every synthetic value and the agency responsible for supplying real data.

login.title: Admin sign-in

errors.config: Admin access is not configured. Set ADMIN_PASSWORD before signing in.

login.environmentNote: Admin access requires the {passwordVar} environment variable. Production deployments should also set {sessionVar}.
